definition: Mag-7 faces 400-700bp operating margin compression by 2027-2029 from three sources: depreciation cliff ($70-120B from $500B+ cumulative AI capex), useful-life extension exhaustion ($8-12B annual boost ending), and energy cost escalation. Current consensus 15-18% EPS CAGR for 7-10 years is inconsistent with this magnitude unless revenue exceeds expectations by 400-700bp annually.
evidence: Depreciation cliff from $500B+ cumulative AI capex creates $70-120B headwind
Useful-life extension accounting boost of $8-12B annually is ending
Not challenged on accounting mechanics in pair_2 debate
open_questions: Whether revenue growth can offset 400-700bp margin compression
Precise timing of depreciation cliff impact
Whether useful-life assumptions get further extended
